name: aiodocker

metadata.name: aiodocker
metadata.summary: A simple Docker HTTP API wrapper written with asyncio and aiohttp.

documentation:
# AsyncIO bindings for docker.io

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/aiodocker.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://badge.fury.io/py/aiodocker
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/aiodocker.svg
:alt: Python Versions
:target: https://pypi.org/project/aiodocker/
```

```{image} https://github.com/aio-libs/aiodocker/actions/workflows/ci-cd.yml/badge.svg?branch=master
:alt: GitHub Actions status for the main branch
:target: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiodocker/actions/workflows/ci-cd.yml?query=branch%3Amaster
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/aio-libs/aiodocker/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Code Coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/aio-libs/aiodocker
```

```{image} https://badges.gitter.im/Join%20Chat.svg
:alt: Chat on Gitter
:target: https://gitter.im/aio-libs/Lobby
```

A simple Docker HTTP API wrapper written with asyncio and aiohttp.

## Installation

```sh
pip install aiodocker
```

## Development

Create a virtualenv (either using `python -m venv`, `pyenv` or your
favorite tools) and install in the editable mode with `ci` and `dev` optional
dependency sets.

```sh
pip install -U pip
pip install -e '.[ci,dev]'  # in zsh, you need to escape brackets
pre-commit install
```

### Running tests

```sh
# Run all tests
make test

# Run individual tests
python -m pytest tests/test_images.py
```

### Building packages

NOTE: Usually you don't need to run this step by yourself.

```sh
pip install -U build
python -m build --sdist --wheel
```

## Documentation

<http://aiodocker.readthedocs.io>

## Examples

```python
import asyncio
import aiodocker

async def list_things(docker):
    print('== Images ==')
    for image in (await docker.images.list()):
        tags = image['RepoTags'][0] if image['RepoTags'] else ''
        print(image['Id'], tags)
    print('== Containers ==')
    for container in (await docker.containers.list()):
        print(f" {container._id}")

async def run_container(docker):
    print('== Running a hello-world container ==')
    container = await docker.containers.create_or_replace(
        config={
            'Cmd': ['/bin/ash', '-c', 'echo "hello world"'],
            'Image': 'alpine:latest',
        },
        name='testing',
    )
    await container.start()
    logs = await container.log(stdout=True)
    print(''.join(logs))
    await container.delete(force=True)

async def main():
    docker = aiodocker.Docker()
    await list_things(docker)
    await run_container(docker)
    await docker.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

methods.name: aiodocker.events.DockerEvents.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribes to the Docker events channel. Use the keyword argument
create_task=False to prevent automatically spawning the background
tasks that listen to the events.

This function returns a ChannelSubscriber object.

methods.name: aiodocker.events.DockerEvents.run
documentation:
Query the events endpoint of the Docker daemon.

Publish messages inside the asyncio queue.

methods.name: aiodocker.swarm.DockerSwarm.init
documentation:
Initialize a new swarm.

Args:
    ListenAddr: listen address used for inter-manager communication
    AdvertiseAddr: address advertised to other nodes.
    DataPathPort: address or interface to use for data path traffic
    DefaultAddrPool: default subnet pools for global scope networks
    SubnetSize: subnet size of the networks created from the default subnet pool
    ForceNewCluster: Force creation of a new swarm.
    SwarmSpec: User modifiable swarm configuration.

Returns:
    id of the swarm node

methods.name: aiodocker.swarm.DockerSwarm.inspect
documentation:
Inspect a swarm.

Returns:
    Info about the swarm

methods.name: aiodocker.swarm.DockerSwarm.join
documentation:
Join a swarm.

Args:
    listen_addr
        Used for inter-manager communication

    advertise_addr
        Externally reachable address advertised to other nodes.

    data_path_addr
        Address or interface to use for data path traffic.

    remote_addrs
        Addresses of manager nodes already participating in the swarm.

    join_token
        Secret token for joining this swarm.

methods.name: aiodocker.swarm.DockerSwarm.leave
documentation:
Leave a swarm.

Args:
    force: force to leave the swarm even if the node is a master

methods.name: aiodocker.networks.DockerNetworks.list
documentation:
Return a list of networks

Args:
    filters: a dict with a list of filters

Available filters:
    dangling=<boolean>
    driver=<driver-name>
    id=<network-id>
    label=<key> or label=<key>=<value> of a network label.
    name=<network-name>
    scope=["swarm"|"global"|"local"]
    type=["custom"|"builtin"]

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.parse_result
documentation:
Convert the response to native objects by the given response type
or the auto-detected HTTP content-type.
It also ensures release of the response object.

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.parse_content_type
documentation:
Decompose the value of HTTP "Content-Type" header into
the main/sub MIME types and other extra options as a dictionary.
All parsed values are lower-cased automatically.

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.clean_map
documentation:
Return a new copied dictionary without the keys with `None` values from
the given Mapping object.

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.format_env
documentation: Formats envs from {key:value} to ['key=value']

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.clean_networks
documentation:
Cleans the values inside `networks`
Returns a new list

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.clean_filters
documentation:
Ensures that the values inside `filters` are lists of string values, by
wrapping scalar values as a single-item lists.  Returns the result as the
jsonized form of `map[string][]string` as described in
https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.29/#operation/ServiceList .

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.mktar_from_dockerfile
documentation:
Create a zipped tar archive from a Dockerfile
**Remember to close the file object**
Args:
    fileobj: a Dockerfile
Returns:
    a NamedTemporaryFile() object

functions.name: aiodocker.utils.compose_auth_header
documentation:
Validate and compose base64-encoded authentication header
with an optional support for parsing legacy-style "user:password"
strings.

Args:
    auth: Authentication information
    registry_addr: An address of the registry server

Returns:
    A base64-encoded X-Registry-Auth header value

classes.name: aiodocker.utils._DecodeHelper
documentation: Decode logs from the Docker Engine

methods.name: aiodocker.docker.Docker._query
documentation:
Get the response object by performing the HTTP request.
The caller is responsible to finalize the response object
via the async context manager protocol.

methods.name: aiodocker.docker.Docker._query_json
documentation: A shorthand of _query() that treats the input as JSON.

methods.name: aiodocker.docker.Docker._query_chunked_post
documentation: A shorthand for uploading data by chunks

methods.name: aiodocker.docker.Docker._docker_machine_ssl_context
documentation: Create a SSLContext object using DOCKER_* env vars.

methods.name: aiodocker.system.DockerSystem.info
documentation:
Get system information, similar to `docker info`.

Returns:

documentation:
Return a list of swarm's nodes.

Args:

Available filters:
    id=<node id>

    membership=(accepted|pending)`

Inspect a node

Args:

documentation:
Update the spec of a node.

Args:

    version: version number of the node being updated

methods.name: aiodocker.nodes.DockerSwarmNodes.remove
documentation:
Remove a node from a swarm.

Args:

Args:

Available filters:

methods.name: aiodocker.secrets.DockerSecrets.create
documentation:
Create a secret

Args:
    name: name of the secret
    labels: user-defined key/value metadata
    data: data to store as secret
    b64: True if data is already Base64-url-safe-encoded
    driver: Driver represents a driver (network, logging, secrets).
    templating: Driver represents a driver (network, logging, secrets).

Returns:
    a dict with info of the created secret

methods.name: aiodocker.secrets.DockerSecrets.inspect
documentation:
Inspect a secret

Args:
    secret_id: ID of the secret

Returns:
    a dict with info about a secret

methods.name: aiodocker.secrets.DockerSecrets.delete
documentation:
Remove a secret

Args:
    secret_id: ID of the secret

Returns:
    True if successful

methods.name: aiodocker.secrets.DockerSecrets.update
documentation:
Update a secret.

Args:
    secret_id: ID of the secret.
    name: name of the secret
    labels: user-defined key/value metadata
    data: data to store as secret
    b64: True if data is already Base64-url-safe-encoded
    driver: Driver represents a driver (network, logging, secrets).
    templating: Driver represents a driver (network, logging, secrets).

Returns:

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.list
documentation:
Return a list of services

Args:
    filters: a dict with a list of filters

Available filters:
    id=<service id>
    label=<service label>
    mode=["replicated"|"global"]
    name=<service name>

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.create
documentation:
Create a service

Args:
    task_template: user modifiable task configuration
    name: name of the service
    labels: user-defined key/value metadata
    mode: scheduling mode for the service
    update_config: update strategy of the service
    rollback_config: rollback strategy of the service
    networks: array of network names/IDs to attach the service to
    endpoint_spec: ports to expose
    auth: authentication information, can be a string, dict or bytes
    registry: used when auth is specified, it provides domain/IP of
        the registry without a protocol

Returns:
    a dict with info of the created service

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.update
documentation:
Update a service.
If rollback is True image will be ignored.

Args:
    service_id: ID or name of the service.
    version: Version of the service that you want to update.
    rollback: Rollback the service to the previous service spec.

Returns:
    True if successful.

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.delete
documentation:
Remove a service

Args:
    service_id: ID or name of the service

Returns:
    True if successful

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.inspect
documentation:
Inspect a service

Args:
    service_id: ID or name of the service

Returns:
    a dict with info about a service

methods.name: aiodocker.services.DockerServices.logs
documentation:
Retrieve logs of the given service

Args:
    details: show service context and extra details provided to logs
    follow: return the logs as a stream.
    stdout: return logs from stdout
    stderr: return logs from stderr
    since: return logs since this time, as a UNIX timestamp
    timestamps: add timestamps to every log line
    is_tty: the service has a pseudo-TTY allocated
    tail: only return this number of log lines
          from the end of the logs, specify as an integer
          or `all` to output all log lines.

classes.name: aiodocker.execs.Exec

methods.name: aiodocker.execs.Exec.start
documentation:
Start this exec instance.
Args:
    timeout: The timeout in seconds for the request to start the exec instance.
    detach: Indicates whether we should detach from the command (like the `-d`
        option to `docker exec`).
    tty: Indicates whether a TTY should be allocated (like the `-t` option to
        `docker exec`).
Returns:
    If `detach` is `True`, this method will return the result of the exec
    process as a binary string.
    If `detach` is False, an `aiohttp.ClientWebSocketResponse` will be returned.
    You can use it to send and receive data the same wa as the response of
    "ws_connect" of aiohttp. If `tty` is `False`, then the messages returned
    from `receive*` will have their `extra` attribute set to 1 if the data was
    from stdout or 2 if from stderr.
When a Tty is allocated for an "exec" operation, the stdout and stderr are streamed
straight to the client.
When a Tty is NOT allocated, then stdout and stderr are multiplexed using the format
given at
https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.40/#operation/ContainerAttach under the "Stream
Format" heading. Note that that documentation is for "docker attach" but the format
also applies to "docker exec."

methods.name: aiodocker.tasks.DockerTasks.list
documentation:
Return a list of tasks

Args:
    filters: a collection of filters

Available filters:
desired-state=(running | shutdown | accepted)
id=<task id>
label=key or label="key=value"
name=<task name>
node=<node id or name>
service=<service name>

methods.name: aiodocker.tasks.DockerTasks.inspect
documentation:
Return info about a task

Args:
    task_id: is ID of the task

methods.name: aiodocker.containers.DockerContainers.run
documentation:
Create and start a container.

If container.start() will raise an error the exception will contain
a `container_id` attribute with the id of the container.

Use `auth` for specifying credentials for pulling absent image from
a private registry.

methods.name: aiodocker.containers.DockerContainers.exec
documentation: Return Exec instance for already created exec object.

methods.name: aiodocker.containers.DockerContainer.commit
documentation:
Commit a container to an image. Similar to the `docker commit`
command.

methods.name: aiodocker.stream.Stream.read_out
documentation: Read from stdout or stderr.

methods.name: aiodocker.stream.Stream.write_in
documentation: Write into stdin.

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.list
documentation: List of images

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.inspect
documentation:
Return low-level information about an image

Args:
    name: name of the image

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.pull
documentation:
Similar to `docker pull`, pull an image locally

Args:
    fromImage: name of the image to pull
    repo: repository name given to an image when it is imported
    tag: if empty when pulling an image all tags
         for the given image to be pulled
    platform: platform in the format `os[/arch[/variant]]`
    auth: special {'auth': base64} pull private repo

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.tag
documentation:
Tag the given image so that it becomes part of a repository.

Args:
    name: name/id of the image to be tagged
    repo: the repository to tag in
    tag: the name for the new tag

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.delete
documentation:
Remove an image along with any untagged parent
images that were referenced by that image

Args:
    name: name/id of the image to delete
    force: remove the image even if it is being used
           by stopped containers or has other tags
    noprune: don't delete untagged parent images

Returns:
    List of deleted images

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.build
documentation:
Build an image given a remote Dockerfile
or a file object with a Dockerfile inside

Args:
    path_dockerfile: path within the build context to the Dockerfile
    remote: a Git repository URI or HTTP/HTTPS context URI
    tag: a name and optional tag to apply to the image
    quiet: suppress verbose build output
    nocache: do not use the cache when building the image
    rm: remove intermediate containers after a successful build
    pull: downloads any updates to the FROM image in Dockerfiles
    encoding: set `Content-Encoding` for the file object your send
    forcerm: always remove intermediate containers, even upon failure
    labels: arbitrary key/value labels to set on the image
    platform: platform in the format `os[/arch[/variant]]`
    fileobj: a tar archive compressed or not

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.export_image
documentation:
Get a tarball of an image by name or id.

Args:
    name: name/id of the image to be exported

Returns:
    Streamreader of tarball image

methods.name: aiodocker.images.DockerImages.import_image
documentation:
Import tarball of image to docker.

Args:
    data: tarball data of image to be imported

Returns:
    Tarball of the image
